title: systemctl link ./*.container is not a valid unit name (systemd podman integration)
body:
### Issue Description

@YHNdnzj from https://github.com/systemd/systemd redirected me to report this bug here because allegedly this a Podman issue? :eyes: 

`systemctl link` complains that `*.container` files are not valid unit names.

Original issue: https://github.com/systemd/systemd/issues/32543

Podman 4.9.3
Ubuntu 24.04
SystemD 255
Kernel 6.8


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Install podman then try to `systemctl --user link ...` any `.container` unit.

### Describe the results you received

```
gnat@gnat:~/Projects/systemd$ systemctl --user link ./*.container
Failed to link unit: "/home/gnat/Projects/systemd/memcached.container" is not a valid unit name.
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
gnat@gnat:~/Projects/systemd$ systemctl --user link ./*.container
Created symlink /home/gnat/.config/containers/systemd/user/memcached.container → /home/gnat/Projects/systemd/memcached.container.
```

### podman info output

```yaml

```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
.container files are not real systemd units so you cannot use any systemd commands on them. Second even if it would work the location would be wrong, the qualdet files must be in our custom location https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#podman-rootless-unit-search-path not the systemd ones so how should systemd know this.

So this cannot be fixed you must create the links manually
